title: Microprogramming
summary: Introduces microprogramming as an alternative sequential circuit implementation: the combinational part is replaced by a ROM whose contents encode the transition table.
content_points: ROM replaces gate-level combinational logic: address = state + inputs; ROM data = next state + outputs.
Advantage: modifying the automaton only requires rewriting the ROM.
Drawback: ROM circuits are slower than dedicated gate implementations.
Example: 4-bit counter needs 2^4=16 ROM locations, each 4 bits wide.
learning_objectives: Explain the microprogramming approach to sequential circuit design
Describe the ROM contents for a given FSM
